mechanism_narrative: Low and stable inflation, as indicated by CPIAUCSL, provides a favorable environment for equity valuations by reducing uncertainty and supporting consumer purchasing power, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 20-day horizon.
failure_condition: SPX 20d returns are consistently negative or flat when CPIAUCSL is stable and low, or a significant positive correlation is not observed over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A significant increase in the unemployment rate (UNRATE) above a certain threshold signals a weakening labor market, which typically precedes or accompanies economic downturns, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: SPX 60d returns are positive or flat in more than 50% of instances where UNRATE rises by more than 0.5 percentage points over three months.

mechanism_narrative: Strong retail sales (RSAFS) combined with robust industrial production (INDPRO) indicates broad-based economic strength, suggesting healthy corporate earnings and driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 20-day horizon.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS and INDPRO both show positive MoM growth
failure_condition: SPX 20d returns are negative or flat when both RSAFS and INDPRO exhibit positive month-over-month growth for two consecutive months.

mechanism_narrative: During periods of aggressive monetary tightening (rising FEDFUNDS), higher borrowing costs and reduced liquidity negatively impact corporate profitability and equity valuations, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: SPX 60d returns are positive or flat in more than 40% of instances where FEDFUNDS increases by more than 0.25 percentage points in a month during a tightening cycle.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained increase in housing starts (HOUST) indicates strong consumer confidence and investment in real estate, which typically has positive spillover effects on the broader economy and corporate earnings, supporting S&P 500 returns over a 5-day horizon.
candidate_trigger: HOUST increases > 5% MoM for 2 consecutive months
failure_condition: SPX 5d returns are negative or flat despite two consecutive months of HOUST increasing by more than 5% MoM.

mechanism_narrative: Robust non-farm payrolls (PAYEMS) growth signifies a healthy and expanding labor market, which translates into higher consumer spending and corporate revenues, thus boosting S&P 500 forward returns over a 20-day horizon.
failure_condition: SPX 20d returns are negative or flat in more than 50% of instances where PAYEMS growth exceeds 150,000 jobs month-over-month.

mechanism_narrative: A significant deceleration or contraction in industrial production (INDPRO) signals weakening manufacturing activity and overall economic output, which negatively impacts corporate profits and S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: SPX 60d returns are positive or flat in more than 40% of instances where INDPRO declines for three consecutive months.

mechanism_narrative: High inflation (CPIAUCSL) combined with a rapidly rising federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS) indicates an environment of stagflationary pressure or aggressive monetary tightening to combat inflation, which is detrimental to equity valuations and leads to negative S&P 500 returns over a 20-day horizon.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL > 4% YoY AND FEDFUNDS rises > 0.5pp in 3 months
failure_condition: SPX 20d returns are positive or flat when CPIAUCSL is above 4% YoY and FEDFUNDS has risen by more than 0.5 percentage points in the last three months.
